title: potentilla hirta subsp. asturica
intro: Potentilla hirta subsp. asturica, a subspecies of the potentilla hirta plant, is a type of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to the Iberian Peninsula in Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Potentilla hirta subsp. asturica include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and possess antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes used in traditional herbal medicine to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues.
